sections.paragraph:
Arab. ران Med. Ye, to conquer, to overcome, followed by على (perhaps kindred to the root רוּם). Not used in Kal, for יָרוּן Pro 29:6, should be referred to רָנַן.

Hithpalel, Psal. 78:65 , בְּגִבֹור מִתְרֹונֵן מִיַּיִן “as a mighty man overcome by wine,” i.e. as in the Vulg. crapulatus a vino; compare the Arabian phrase in Firuzabadi, رانت عليه الخمر wine has overcome him, i.e. he has become drunken; and see the remarks on the Hebrew verbs בָּלַע Niphal, הָלַם, עָבַר No. 5, a.
